hypothesis: If a computational framework combining fragment-based polymer generation, DFT-refined force field parameters, and Bayesian optimization with pre-trained molecular embeddings is applied to the search space of hypothetical polyamines, then it will accelerate the discovery of high-performance solid-state CO2 capture materials beyond what is achievable through exhaustive simulation alone, because the surrogate model-guided active learning efficiently navigates the chemical space by exploiting structural representations to prioritize candidates with high CO2 adsorption capacity while minimizing the number of costly GCMC simulations required.
